commits.messageBody:
A crashed or killed MastraCode used to block the next launch with an opaque
HarnessSessionLockedError because the dead process left its v1 session lease
in storage until TTL. The runtime now catches that, waits for the lease to
expire (up to 60s on startup, 2s mid-session), and adopts the previous
session once acquirable, emitting info status messages while it retries.

After 5s of waiting on a TTY, an interactive (W)ait / (F)orce-claim /
(N)ew thread / (Q)uit prompt is shown with a 30s response budget so an
unattended terminal still recovers automatically.

MASTRACODE_LEASE_RECOVERY=wait|force-claim|new-thread|quit skips the
prompt programmatically. Force-claim semantics persist across owner-change
races. On exhaustion the runtime throws a new MastraCodeSessionLeaseRecoveryError
(exported from mastracode) carrying the underlying HarnessSessionLockedError
as cause.

Also fixes a related terminal-state bug surfaced by the same crash scenario:
pi-tui's extended keyboard modes used to stick around after a SIGKILL'd
mastracode, leaving the parent shell stuck emitting raw CSI-u keypress
sequences. main.ts now emits the restore sequence on every trappable exit
signal and once at launch so the next invocation heals any prior breakage.

Closes MASTRA-4344.
commits.messageHeadline: fix(mastracode): recover from stale Harness v1 session leases on startup

title: fix(mastracode): recover from stale Harness v1 session leases on startup
